articles.description: A sea slug smaller than a sesame seed has turned up in Taiwan’s coastal waters — and it’s so tiny and unusual that scientists realized they had discovered a completely new species. articles.title: NASA’s Webb telescope discovers a planet where rock clouds vanish every night
articles.description: A giant planet nearly 700 light-years away has a bizarre daily weather cycle where mineral clouds appear every morning and vanish by nightfall. articles.title: NASA’s Fermi telescope reveals the power source behind monster supernovae
articles.description: NASA’s Fermi telescope has detected what may be the first confirmed gamma-ray signal from a superluminous supernova — one of the most extreme explosions in the universe. Scientists believe the blast was powered by a rapidly spinning magnetar, an exotic neutron star with unbelievably strong magnetic

articles.title: One killed and nine missing after chemical explosion at US paper mill
articles.description: Nine others were injured and another nine are missing after a tank ruptured at a paper mill in Longview, Washington.

articles.title: Scientists discover ancient single-celled ancestors still live on in your blood
articles.description: Scientists uncovered evidence that human blood cells may trace their origins back to single-celled ancestors that lived 700 million years ago. By rebuilding the evolutionary family tree of blood cells, the team revealed how today’s immune system grew from some of Earth’s earliest life forms.
